item.drivebysable.cable.tooltip.summary: Connects _redstone input sources_ to any number of _outputs_
item.drivebysable.cable_cutter.tooltip.c: When R-Clicked on a Cable Source
item.drivebysable.cable_cutter.tooltip.b: Removes all _cable connections_ sourced from the targeted block.
Retains all the features of the _Linked Typewriter_ while also integrating with _cable networks_.
_Toggles_ the Typewriter Cable Hub. _Movement controls_ and any _assigned keys_ are taken over while it's active.
Binds the _Linked Controller's_ frequencies to the typewriter.
Outputs a _redstone signal_ on the matching _cable channel_ whenever that key is pressed.
Saves _same-sublevel cable connections_ into _schematics_ and restores them when the schematic is loaded
drivebysable.ponder.tag.main.description: Cable-based redstone networking, no frequencies required
drivebysable.ponder.cable_hub_intro.head: Using a Cable Hub
drivebysable.ponder.cable_hub_intro.text: The Cable Hub can link multiple channels to redstone signal outputs
Use a Cable on the Hub block to enter setup mode
Then click any surface to bind an output to the selected channel
Scroll to change the selected channel
Use the Cable on the hub again to exit setup mode
Connect a Linked Controller to the Hub to access inputs
drivebysable.ponder.cable_hub_lectern.he: Binding a Lectern Controller to a Cable Hub
drivebysable.ponder.cable_hub_lectern.te: Cable Hub networks also respect bound Lectern Controllers
First, set up a cable network
Then, bind a Linked Controller to the Cable Hub
And place the bound controller in a Lectern
drivebysable.ponder.adv_hub_intro.text_1: This is an Advanced Cable Hub
drivebysable.ponder.adv_hub_intro.text_2: Similar to the standard Cable Hub, it can be used to create cable networks
drivebysable.ponder.adv_hub_intro.text_3: The Advanced Hub has more channel options, and is meant to be used with a configured Tweaked Controller
drivebysable.ponder.adv_hub_intro.text_4: Setting up networks works the same as before
drivebysable.ponder.adv_hub_intro.text_5: Use a Cable on the Hub block to enter setup mode
drivebysable.ponder.adv_hub_intro.text_6: Then click any surface to bind an output to the selected channel
drivebysable.ponder.adv_hub_intro.text_7: Scroll to change the selected channel
drivebysable.ponder.adv_hub_intro.text_8: Use the Cable on the hub again to exit setup mode
drivebysable.ponder.adv_hub_intro.text_9: Connect a configured Tweaked Controller to the hub to access inputs
drivebysable.ponder.adv_hub_intro.text_1: If the controller is configured with a gamepad, the Advanced Hub will transmit the appropriate signal strengths
The Advanced Hub will also transmit static inputs as normal
drivebysable.ponder.advanced_cable_hub_l: Binding a Tweaked Lectern Controller to an Advanced Cable Hub
Advanced Cable Hub networks also respect bound Tweaked Lectern Controllers
First, set up a cable network
Then, bind a Tweaked Controller to the Advanced Cable Hub
And place the bound controller in a Lectern
drivebysable.ponder.typewriter_hub_intro: This is a Typewriter Cable Hub
As a modified Linked Typewriter, it can be used with Redstone Link frequencies, and as a cable network source
Right-click while Sneaking to open the link frequency configuration interface
Alternatively, use the Typewriter Hub on any link to bind it to a key
Frequencies can also be set with a Linked Controller
Use the configured Linked Controller on the Typewriter Hub to import its frequencies
Setting up cable networks works the same as before
Use a Cable on the Hub block to enter setup mode
Then click any surface to bind an output to the selected channel
Use the Cable on the hub again to exit setup mode
drivebysable.configuration.shouldConsume: Whether or not wires should be consumed upon use when in survival mode.
